summary: Israel’s military reported destroying four Hamas rocket launch positions in Gaza during the cease‑fire. Iran’s deputy foreign minister said there is no plan for IAEA inspectors to access nuclear sites damaged in recent US‑Israeli strikes. Hezbollah‑linked tensions persist as Israel vows not to withdraw from southern Lebanon despite U.S. pressure.
key_points: Israel’s IDF struck and destroyed four rocket launch sites in Gaza that were set up during the cease‑fire (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
Iran’s deputy foreign minister stated there is no plan for IAEA inspectors to access Iran’s nuclear facilities damaged by US‑Israeli attacks (Source: @presstv/195453).
Israel’s defence minister warned that the leader of Hamas will be eliminated if targeted (Source: @amitsegal/62491).
Hezbollah‑linked forces in Lebanon reportedly detonated an explosive device in the MAG field in Naameh (Source: @redlinkleb/28045).
Pakistan’s prime minister announced a visit to Tehran to pay respects to Iran’s late Supreme Leader (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4240).
Iran’s central bank governor said Iran can receive oil export payments in any currency it chooses (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4248).
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed a U.S.‑Iran memorandum of understanding on safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz with UAE officials (Source: @VahidOnline/76650).
Israel’s ambassador to Lebanon noted difficulties in negotiations but hopes they will be resolved today (Source: @yediotnews25/131885).

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s IDF destroyed four Hamas rocket launch positions in Gaza during the cease‑fire, reducing immediate threat to Israeli forces. Iran rejected any IAEA inspection of its nuclear sites damaged in recent US‑Israeli strikes, heightening nuclear tension. Hezbollah‑linked activity in southern Lebanon continues, with Israel pledging no withdrawal despite diplomatic pressure.

points: Iran’s deputy foreign minister said there is no plan for IAEA inspectors to access nuclear sites damaged by US‑Israeli attacks, indicating resistance to international oversight (Source: @presstv/195453).
Iran’s central bank governor announced that Iran can receive oil export payments in any currency, signaling flexibility in circumventing sanctions (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4248).
PressTV reported Iran has drafted a proposal to create a radio‑TV union within the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation, reflecting a push for regional media cooperation (Source: @presstv/195456).
Pakistan’s prime minister Shehbaz Sharif will visit Tehran next week to reaffirm solidarity with Iran, underscoring diplomatic ties (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4240).

The IDF reported destroying four rocket launch sites in Gaza that were established during the cease‑fire, describing them as immediate threats to Israeli troops and civilians (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
Israel’s defence minister warned that the Hamas leader will be eliminated if targeted, indicating a policy of high‑value targeting (Source: @amitsegal/62491).
Israel’s chief of staff met with senior rabbis to discuss recruitment and integration of religious soldiers, highlighting manpower challenges in the ongoing war (Source: @New_security8200/202072).
Israel’s ambassador to Lebanon said negotiations are difficult but hopes for resolution today, reflecting ongoing diplomatic efforts (Source: @yediotnews25/131885).
IDF operations in Gaza targeted four newly‑built Hamas rocket launch positions during the cease‑fire, aiming to prevent future attacks (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
No specific incidents were reported from the West Bank in the provided messages.
Hezbollah‑linked forces reportedly detonated an explosive device in the MAG field in Naameh, indicating active militant operations (Source: @redlinkleb/28045).

Israel’s security minister stated the country will not withdraw from southern Lebanon even if the United States requests it, and will push ground forces toward Beirut (Source: @rodast_omiddana/74310).
U.S.‑Lebanon negotiations face challenges, but the U.S. ambassador expressed hope for progress (Source: @yediotnews25/131885).
No explicit operational information about Syria or Iraq appears in the supplied messages.

U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed a U.S.–Iran agreement on safe ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz with UAE officials, indicating diplomatic engagement (Source: @VahidOnline/76650).
Trump claimed Iran told the U.S. it would not charge fees for ships passing the Strait of Hormuz; he warned negotiations would end if the claim were false (Source: @yediotnews25/131872).

key_developments: Israel’s IDF destroyed four Hamas rocket launch sites in Gaza during the cease‑fire, removing immediate launch capability (Source: @idf_telegram/24429).
Iran’s deputy foreign minister confirmed no IAEA inspection plans for nuclear sites damaged by recent attacks, maintaining nuclear opacity (Source: @presstv/195453).
Hezbollah‑linked explosives were detonated in Naameh, Lebanon, showing continued militant activity (Source: @redlinkleb/28045).
Israel’s defence minister warned the Hamas leader will be eliminated if targeted, indicating escalation in leadership targeting (Source: @amitsegal/62491).
Pakistan’s prime minister will visit Tehran next week, reinforcing Iran‑Pakistan diplomatic ties (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4240).
Iran’s central bank governor announced acceptance of oil payments in any currency, suggesting sanctions‑evasion strategies (Source: @Faytuks_Network/4248).
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed a U.S.–Iran memorandum on safe Hormuz passage with UAE officials, reflecting diplomatic outreach (Source: @VahidOnline/76650).
sections.threat_assessment: The destruction of Hamas rocket launch sites in Gaza reduces immediate fire risk but underscores Israel’s intent to neutralize launch capabilities during cease‑fire periods. Iran’s refusal to allow IAEA inspections after recent strikes raises the risk of nuclear escalation, while its flexible oil payment policy suggests attempts to bypass sanctions. Hezbollah’s explosive activity in southern Lebanon, coupled with Israel’s refusal to withdraw, maintains a volatile front that could spark broader conflict.
sections.regional_response: Pakistan’s planned visit to Tehran signals continued regional support for Iran, potentially bolstering Tehran’s diplomatic standing. The United States is engaging Gulf partners, as seen in discussions with the UAE on Hormuz security, while also confronting Iranian claims about shipping fees. Israel’s internal mobilization efforts, including outreach to religious communities, aim to sustain manpower amid prolonged operations.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian nuclear site conditions is critical given the lack of IAEA access; satellite imagery should focus on known facilities. Continued surveillance of southern Lebanon is required to track Hezbollah‑linked explosives and Israeli force movements. Tracking financial flows related to Iran’s oil payments in alternative currencies may reveal sanction‑evasion networks. Israeli targeting of Hamas leadership suggests future high‑value strikes, necessitating HUMINT on leadership locations.
